methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by J.B. Straubel that has raised $2.3B+ and carries a current post-money valuation of $6.0B+. The valuation landscape includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers across battery, hydrogen, and fusion technology subsectors. The most relevant private comparables—TAE Technologies, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, QuantumScape, and Form Energy—span similar or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implying a valuation of $9.0B based on the $6.0B last post-money. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (KoBold Metals at $2.0B, Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B, and Amogy at $700M) yields a median of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $2.0B. The divergence between these methods reflects the substantial scale premium Redwood commands relative to earlier-stage climate tech peers.

Triangulated analysis supports a range of $850M to $10.3B, with a median of $5.0B. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around the sustainability of Redwood's current valuation relative to peer-set norms and the timing and magnitude of future financing. The stage-progression method anchors the upside; peer comparables suggest meaningful downside risk absent demonstrable revenue or efficiency milestones that would justify the $6B+ entry valuation.
